title: Best Custom Apparel for Food Trucks & Mobile Food Vendors
metaDescription: Branded tees, caps, and hoodies for food trucks and mobile food vendors. Merch your customers actually buy at the window, decorated in-house in Milton, WA.
bodyMarkdown:
A food truck lives or dies on brand recognition. Your logo, your colors, and that one dish everyone posts about are the whole business. So the apparel your crew wears at the window isn't just a uniform. It's a rolling billboard, and if you play it right, it's a second revenue stream. The customers who wait twenty minutes for your birria are the same ones who'll happily buy a tee with your art on it.

At Northwest Custom Apparel we've been decorating gear for Pacific Northwest small businesses since 1977, right here in Milton, WA. We print and embroider everything in-house, which means we can match your brand colors, turn around a small crew's worth of shirts fast, and print merch runs bold enough to sell. Here's how to build a food-truck apparel program that works the crew and the window at the same time.

## Merch Your Customers Actually Buy

The single best marketing dollar a food truck spends is on a soft, wearable tee with killer art. Fans want to represent the truck they love, and every shirt out in the wild is a walking recommendation. The trick is picking a blank that feels good enough that people wear it for real, not just once.

For window merch and small retail runs, we lean on full-color DTG and screen print so your illustrated logo, mascot, or signature dish comes through with the color it deserves.

- [Bella+Canvas BC3001](/product.html?style=BC3001) — the go-to retail tee. Soft, fitted, and the blank customers happily buy at the window. Great for detailed DTG art and a wide color range. See the full [Bella+Canvas lineup](/custom-bella-canvas).
- [Port & Company PC61](/product.html?style=PC61) — a heavier, durable crew tee that's perfect for staff shirts and value-priced merch runs when you need volume. Browse [Port & Company](/custom-port-and-company).

Print a couple of designs, keep a small bin of sizes in the truck, and you've got merch selling itself while the line waits.

## Caps and Hoodies for the Crew (and the Fans)

Your crew is on camera all day, whether they know it or not. A branded cap ties the whole look together and reads instantly in photos and reels. Caps are also one of the easiest merch items to sell, because everyone wears them and one size fits most.

- [Richardson 112](/product.html?style=112) — the trucker cap that basically defined the category. Structured front, mesh back, and an embroidered or patched logo that looks sharp on staff and sells to fans. More at [custom Richardson](/custom-richardson).
- [Port & Company PC90H](/product.html?style=PC90H) — a heavyweight crew hoodie for cold-morning prep, market season, and PNW drizzle. Warm, roomy, and a great canvas for a big front or back print. See [Port & Company](/custom-port-and-company).

Hoodies in particular do double duty here: your crew stays warm at the 6 a.m. load-in, and they're a premium merch item when the weather turns, which around Milton is most of the year.

## Build a Program, Not a One-Off

The smartest food-truck owners treat apparel like a menu: a couple of core staff pieces, plus a small rotating merch line. Start with crew tees and caps for the team, add a fan tee in your best-selling colorway, and drop a hoodie for fall and winter. Keep the art consistent so every piece reinforces the same brand.

A few things that keep it easy:

- **Order in the right quantities.** Small crew? No problem. We handle short runs and can scale up when a design takes off.
- **Match your colors.** Bring your logo and brand colors and we'll get the print dialed in in-house.
- **Yes, we make aprons too.** Front-of-house aprons round out the look — [request a quote](/pages/request-a-quote.html) and we'll add them to the order. (Our apparel program is the focus here; aprons are a quick add-on.)

Whether you're a one-truck operation or running a small fleet across the South Sound, we'll help you build gear that outfits the crew and moves at the window.

Ready to put your art on shirts, caps, and hoodies people actually want to wear? [Request a quote](/pages/request-a-quote.html) and let's build your food-truck apparel program together.

title: Best Custom Apparel for Marinas, Boating & Watersports Businesses
metaDescription: Custom staff apparel and marina merch built for sun, water, and long days on the dock. See our top picks for boat rentals, yacht clubs, and charters.
bodyMarkdown:
Running a marina, boat rental, yacht club, or charter operation in the Pacific Northwest means your crew is out in the elements all day. Sun glare off Puget Sound, salt spray, wind, and the occasional surprise squall are all part of the job. The apparel your staff wears has to keep up, and the merch you sell in the ship's store has to be something visitors actually want to take home.

At Northwest Custom Apparel in Milton, WA, we have been decorating apparel for local businesses since 1977, and waterfront operations are one of our favorite jobs. Here is how we help marinas and watersports outfitters look sharp on the dock and move product in the store.

## Staff Apparel That Handles Sun and Water

The single most important thing for dock crew, dockhands, fuel attendants, and charter captains is a performance polo that breathes and dries fast. Cotton soaks up sweat and spray and stays heavy all day. Moisture-wicking performance fabric pulls that moisture off the body and dries in the breeze, which matters a lot when someone is bending over cleats and hauling lines for eight hours.

A clean embroidered logo on the left chest reads as professional and holds up wash after wash, which is exactly what you want for a uniform program. Here are the staff pieces we recommend most for waterfront work:

- [Sport-Tek ST650](/product.html?style=ST650) — Our go-to moisture-wicking performance polo. Snag-resistant, breathable, and dries quickly, so it is ideal for sun-exposed dock crew and fuel dock attendants.
- [OGIO OG101](/product.html?style=OG101) — A stretch performance polo with a slightly more upscale look, great for yacht club front-desk staff and charter hosts who greet guests.
- [Port Authority K500](/product.html?style=K500) — A durable silk-touch polo when you want a classic look for office and management staff who are less exposed to spray.
- [CornerStone CSV105](/product.html?style=CSV105) — A Class 2 hi-vis vest for anyone directing traffic in the parking lot, working the launch ramp, or on deck at dusk. Pair it with our [safety-stripe program](/custom-safety-apparel) for extra visibility.

Browse the full lineup on our [Sport-Tek](/custom-sport-tek), [OGIO](/custom-ogio), and [Port Authority](/custom-port-authority) brand pages to compare colors and fits.

## Caps: Sun Protection and Brand Visibility

A branded cap is the hardest-working piece in any marina program. It shades faces from that reflective water glare, and it walks around the dock as free advertising all season. Structured caps hold their shape and take an embroidered logo cleanly.

- [Richardson 112](/product.html?style=112) — The classic trucker cap. The mesh back keeps heads cool, the foam front panel gives your logo a crisp canvas, and it is far and away the most requested cap we run. See more on our [Richardson](/custom-richardson) page.
- [New Era NE1000](/product.html?style=NE1000) — A structured stretch-fit cap with a modern profile for a more premium look, popular with yacht clubs and higher-end charter brands. Explore the [New Era](/custom-new-era) lineup for options.

## Retail Merch for the Marina Store

Visitors, boaters, and weekend renters love picking up a shirt that ties them to the water and to your brand. The trick is stocking a tee that feels good enough that people actually wear it home, not one that ends up as a shop rag.

- [Bella+Canvas BC3001](/product.html?style=BC3001) — A soft, retail-quality ringspun tee with a flattering fit and a deep color range. This is the tee we recommend for any marina store or gift shop that wants merch people will genuinely wear. Print your dock name, a nautical graphic, or a PNW-waterfront design and it sells itself. See the full [Bella+Canvas](/custom-bella-canvas) collection.

For a more budget-friendly bulk option, or for staff giveaways and event tees, our [Gildan](/custom-gildan) and [Port & Company](/custom-port-and-company) lines are dependable workhorses.

## Let's Rig Up Your Program

Whether you need a dialed-in uniform program for the crew, caps for the whole season, or retail tees for the store, we can build a package that fits your operation. As a local Milton, WA shop, we can talk through logo placement, colors, and quantities without the runaround.

Ready to get started? [Request a quote](/pages/request-a-quote.html) and tell us about your marina, and we will help you pick the right pieces and get your logo on the water.

title: Best Custom Apparel for Churches & Ministries
metaDescription: The best custom tees, hoodies, caps and polos for church volunteers, youth groups, VBS and mission trips. Bulk-friendly picks from a Milton, WA decorator.
bodyMarkdown:
Outfitting a church or ministry team is its own kind of project. You're often ordering in bulk, working against a Sunday-morning or camp-week deadline, and you want everyone to look like they belong together the moment a first-time visitor walks in. Whether it's a greeter team, a youth group, a VBS week, or a mission trip, the right shirt does a lot of quiet work: it makes volunteers easy to spot and makes newcomers feel welcomed.

At Northwest Custom Apparel in Milton, WA, we've decorated apparel for congregations and ministries across the Puget Sound since 1977. Here's how we help churches pick garments that are budget-friendly, comfortable, and ready fast.

## Bulk event & volunteer tees

For big counts — think a whole VBS crew, a serve day, or a Sunday event where you're outfitting dozens or hundreds — you want a dependable, affordable tee that screen prints beautifully. Screen printing is the most cost-effective way to decorate large runs, and these blanks are built for it.

- [Port & Company PC61](/product.html?style=PC61) — the workhorse church-event tee: durable cotton fabric, huge color range, and priced for big volunteer orders.
- [Gildan 2000](/product.html?style=2000) — a heavier all-cotton classic that holds a bold print and stands up to repeated washing through camp week and beyond.

Both come in enough colors to match your ministry's branding or to color-code teams (kids in one color, leaders in another). Explore the full lineups on our [Port & Company](/custom-port-and-company) and [Gildan](/custom-gildan) brand pages.

## Softer merch tees for youth groups

When the shirt is something students actually want to wear to school — retreat merch, a youth-group logo, a small-group tee — reach for a softer, more fashion-forward blank. The fit and hand feel matter as much as the design.

- [Bella+Canvas BC3001](/product.html?style=BC3001) — the go-to soft, retail-fit tee. Lightweight, comfortable, and a favorite for youth ministry and modern church merch.

See more on the [Bella+Canvas](/custom-bella-canvas) page. These run a little slimmer than the bulk tees above, so they're great for merch drops and less ideal when you need a true one-size-fits-most volunteer shirt.

## Hoodies, caps & staff polos

Not everything is a tee. Pacific Northwest ministries lean on layers most of the year, and a coordinated staff look goes a long way for greeters and leadership.

- [Port & Company PC90H](/product.html?style=PC90H) — a warm, budget-friendly fleece hoodie that's perfect for youth-group merch, camp staff, and cool Milton mornings.
- [Richardson 112](/product.html?style=112) — the classic trucker cap for volunteer and staff teams; embroider your logo and it instantly reads as "official."
- [Port Authority K500](/product.html?style=K500) — a clean, professional polo for greeters, ushers, and church staff who want to look put-together without a full uniform.

Browse the [Port & Company](/custom-port-and-company), [Richardson](/custom-richardson), and [Port Authority](/custom-port-authority) pages for colors and sizing. A common church setup: soft tees for the congregation and youth, embroidered caps and polos for the people serving and welcoming.

## How to decide (and get it done fast)

A quick way to think about it:

- **Big count, tight budget, one bold logo?** PC61 or Gildan 2000, screen printed.
- **Merch people want to keep wearing?** BC3001.
- **Cool weather or camp staff?** PC90H hoodies.
- **Greeters and leadership who need to stand out?** Richardson 112 caps and K500 polos, embroidered.

For large orders, screen printing gives you the best value — you can start a bulk print order right from our [screen print quote builder](/quote-builders/screenprint-quote-builder.html). Not sure which garment or decoration method fits your event? Tell us your headcount, timeline, and design, and we'll help you land it.

Ready to outfit your team? [Request a quote](/pages/request-a-quote.html) and our Milton, WA crew will get your ministry looking unified and welcoming — on time for Sunday.

title: Best Custom Apparel for Golf Courses & Country Clubs
metaDescription: Custom polos, caps, and pro-shop merch for golf courses and country clubs. Staff uniforms and members-buy-it apparel from a Milton, WA decorator.
bodyMarkdown:
A golf course is really two apparel businesses running side by side. There's the pro shop, where members and guests actually shop for something they'd wear off the course. And there's the crew that keeps the place running — starters, beverage-cart staff, grounds, clubhouse. Both need a clean, consistent look with your logo, and both reward getting the garment choice right the first time.

At Northwest Custom Apparel in Milton, WA, we've decorated for Puget Sound courses and clubs since 1977. In-house embroidery means your crest or wordmark comes back tight and repeatable, order after order — which matters when a member sees the same logo on a starter's chest and then on a polo folded in the shop.

This guide is about the day-to-day club business: merch members will buy and uniforms that hold up. If you're outfitting a scramble or a charity event, that's a different animal — see our [golf tournament apparel program](/golf-tournament-apparel) for one-off event kits.

## Pro-shop merch members will actually buy

The test for anything on your shop wall is simple: would a member pay for it if it didn't have your logo on it? Lead with a premium polo that feels like a name-brand golf shirt, because that's what moves.

- **[TravisMathew TM1MU411](/product.html?style=TM1MU411)** — the flagship. A recognized golf brand with a soft, structured feel that reads "pro shop," not "company picnic." This is the polo members buy in three colors. Explore the full line on our [custom TravisMathew page](/custom-travismathew).
- **[OGIO OG101](/product.html?style=OG101)** — a performance polo with moisture management and a modern athletic cut. Great for the member who wants stretch and breathability on a warm round. See more on the [custom OGIO page](/custom-ogio).
- **[Richardson 112](/product.html?style=112)** — the trucker cap that everyone already owns and wants another of. Structured front, mesh back, embroiders beautifully. A pro-shop staple that practically sells itself. Browse the [custom Richardson page](/custom-richardson).
- **[New Era NE1000](/product.html?style=NE1000)** — a structured stretch-fit cap with a cleaner, more premium look for clubs that want something a step above the trucker. Details on the [custom New Era page](/custom-new-era).

Stock the caps deep. They're the impulse buy at the register and the highest-margin logo item in most shops.

## Staff uniforms that look sharp all season

Uniform polos take a beating — daily wear, washing, sun. You want colorfast, comfortable, and consistent across departments so the whole team reads as one operation. Match the shirt to the role.

- **[TravisMathew TM1MU411](/product.html?style=TM1MU411)** — put your best-dressed people, like the pro and front-desk staff, in the same premium polo you sell. It signals quality the moment a guest walks in.
- **[OGIO OG101](/product.html?style=OG101)** — ideal for starters and beverage-cart staff who are moving and outdoors all day. Performance fabric keeps them comfortable through a full shift.
- **[Port Authority K500](/product.html?style=K500)** — the workhorse value polo for larger crews, grounds, and clubhouse. Durable, tons of colors, and easy to budget when you're outfitting a big team. See the [custom Port Authority page](/custom-port-authority).
- **[Sport-Tek ST650](/product.html?style=ST650)** — a moisture-wicking micropique option when you want performance at a friendlier price than the premium brands. More on the [custom Sport-Tek page](/custom-sport-tek).

A smart move: color-code by department. Grounds in one shade, food-and-beverage in another, pro shop in your flagship polo. Members learn who to ask, and your team looks organized.

## Round it out for the whole property

Weather on a PNW course turns fast, so keep a light layer in the mix for early tee times and shoulder-season staff. Our [custom Sport-Tek](/custom-sport-tek) and [custom Port Authority](/custom-port-authority) lines carry quarter-zips and jackets that pair cleanly with your uniform polos. And if any of your crew works cart paths or the entrance near traffic, ask about [high-visibility safety apparel](/custom-safety-apparel) so grounds staff stay compliant without looking like a construction site.

## Let's build your club's look

Whether you're refreshing the pro-shop wall, standardizing staff uniforms, or both, we'll help you pick the right garments, nail your logo, and keep reorders consistent year to year. Tell us what you're outfitting and we'll put numbers to it — [request a quote](/pages/request-a-quote.html) and a Milton-based team member will get you set up.

title: Best Custom Apparel for Pet Businesses: Groomers, Vets & Dog Daycare
metaDescription: Durable, washable custom apparel for groomers, vets and dog daycare staff — plus retail merch pet parents love. Local Milton, WA decorator since 1977.
bodyMarkdown:
Running a grooming salon, a vet clinic, or a dog daycare means your team's clothes take a beating — fur, water, shampoo, muddy paws, the works. You need staff apparel that looks professional at drop-off and still holds up after a hundred wash cycles. And because pet parents are some of the most loyal customers around, the right branded tee or cap doubles as retail merch they'll happily buy off the front counter.

We've been decorating apparel for Pacific Northwest businesses out of Milton, WA since 1977, and pet-care shops are a favorite of ours. Here's how we'd build a wardrobe that survives the job and builds your brand.

## Staff Polos That Handle Fur and Water

For anyone at the front desk, in the exam room, or on the grooming table, an easy-care polo is the workhorse. It reads clean and approachable — exactly the friendly, professional look pet owners want to hand their dog over to — and it stands up to constant washing.

The [Port Authority K500](/product.html?style=K500) is a snag-resistant, wrinkle-fighting polo built for easy care. It's our top pick around fur, water, and daily washing, and it holds embroidery beautifully. For groomers and handlers who are on their feet and moving all day, the [Sport-Tek ST650](/product.html?style=ST650) is a moisture-wicking performance polo — breathable, quick-drying, and forgiving of the mess.

Embroidered logos look sharp and last on both of these. Browse the full lineups on our [Port Authority](/custom-port-authority) and [Sport-Tek](/custom-sport-tek) brand pages.

## Tees for the Team and the Retail Rack

Tees are where pet businesses really shine. A soft, well-branded shirt works as a daycare uniform, a bath-day t-shirt, and a piece of merch pet parents grab because it features their favorite spot — or a cute paw-print design.

The [Port & Company PC61](/product.html?style=PC61) is a durable, budget-friendly staff tee that takes a beating and keeps its shape — great for high-turnover uniform shirts and bath-day wear. The [Bella+Canvas BC3001](/product.html?style=BC3001) is the soft, retail-quality tee pet parents actually want to wear, perfect for merch you sell at the counter or hand out to loyal clients.

Stock your team in the [Port & Company](/custom-port-and-company) line and put the softer [Bella+Canvas](/custom-bella-canvas) tees on your retail rack — one durable, one premium, both easy to print or embroider.

## Caps to Finish the Look

A branded cap is the easiest way to make a small team look uniform, and it's another merch item customers love — especially for mobile groomers and dog-walking crews out in the field.

The [Richardson 112](/product.html?style=112) is the classic trucker cap, structured and adjustable to fit everyone. It's the go-to for embroidered pet-business logos and sells well on the retail side too. See more color and style options on our [Richardson](/custom-richardson) page.

## A Few Tips for Pet-Care Apparel

- **Prioritize washability.** Between fur, dander, and shampoo, these garments get laundered constantly. Easy-care fabrics and durable decoration matter more than anything.
- **Keep the brand friendly.** Approachable logos, warm colors, and a clean layout tell nervous pet owners they're in good hands.
- **Build in merch from day one.** Order a few extra soft tees and caps to sell up front — pet parents are proud to represent the people who care for their animals.
- **Mix decoration methods.** Embroidery gives polos and caps a polished, professional look; screen print or DTF keeps retail tees affordable at volume.

Whether you're outfitting one groomer or a whole boarding facility, we'll help you pick the right garments, dial in your logo, and keep reorders simple. [Request a quote](/pages/request-a-quote.html) and we'll get your pet business looking its best — mess and all.

title: 2026's Best-Selling Custom Apparel Styles for PNW Businesses
metaDescription: The custom apparel styles PNW businesses order most in 2026 — caps, tees, polos, hoodies, workwear, and hi-vis. Start-here picks from our Milton, WA shop.
bodyMarkdown:
If you're a new business owner staring at thousands of blank garment options, this is the list to start with. At Northwest Custom Apparel in Milton, WA, we've been decorating gear for Puget Sound businesses since 1977, and a handful of styles show up on our order sheets over and over. They're the ones that print clean, hold up to real work and weather, and keep customers coming back for reorders.

Here's the short version: pick a proven blank, and everything downstream gets easier. Below are our most-ordered styles for 2026, grouped by category, so you can build a full uniform or merch lineup without second-guessing the basics.

## Caps and Tees: The Everyday Workhorses

Caps and tees are where most PNW businesses begin. A cap is the fastest way to get your logo out in the field, and tees cover everything from crew shirts to giveaway merch.

- [Richardson 112](/product.html?style=112) — the trucker cap the Northwest runs on; structured front, mesh back, and it takes embroidery beautifully. See the full [Richardson lineup](/custom-richardson).
- [New Era NE1000](/product.html?style=NE1000) — a modern structured cap when you want a step up in look and feel. Browse [custom New Era](/custom-new-era).
- [Bella+Canvas BC3001](/product.html?style=BC3001) — our go-to soft tee for retail-quality merch and staff shirts people actually want to wear. More at [custom Bella+Canvas](/custom-bella-canvas).
- [Port & Company PC61](/product.html?style=PC61) — the durable value tee for uniforms, events, and anything high-volume. See [custom Port & Company](/custom-port-and-company).
- [Gildan 2000](/product.html?style=2000) — the classic bulk tee when you need a lot of shirts and a friendly budget. Browse [custom Gildan](/custom-gildan).

For a soft, fashion-forward option beyond the BC3001, the [District DT6000](/product.html?style=DT6000) is a strong pick too — see [custom District](/custom-district).

## Polos: Clean, Professional, Easy to Reorder

When you want your team to look put-together without going full formal, a polo does the job. It's the default for office staff, service techs, and anyone meeting customers face to face.

- [Sport-Tek ST650](/product.html?style=ST650) — a moisture-wicking sport polo that resists wrinkles and shrugs off a long shift; our most-ordered polo for active teams. See [custom Sport-Tek](/custom-sport-tek).
- [Port Authority K500](/product.html?style=K500) — a classic no-fuss polo that embroiders cleanly and suits almost any industry. Browse [custom Port Authority](/custom-port-authority).

Want something more premium for management or a golf event? Look at [OGIO](/custom-ogio) and [TravisMathew](/custom-travismathew) polos, and check our [golf tournament apparel](/golf-tournament-apparel) program.

## Hoodies and Fleece: PNW Essentials

This is the Pacific Northwest — a good hoodie is basically a uniform requirement nine months of the year. Fleece is one of our most reliable reorder categories.

- [Port & Company PC90H](/product.html?style=PC90H) — a heavyweight pullover hoodie that's warm, durable, and a proven canvas for embroidery or print. See [custom Port & Company](/custom-port-and-company).

For a lighter budget blend, the [Gildan 18500](/product.html?style=18500) is another workhorse — browse [custom Gildan](/custom-gildan).

## Workwear and Hi-Vis: Built for the Job Site

Construction, trades, landscaping, and delivery crews are a huge part of what we do around Milton and the greater Puget Sound. These styles are built to survive the conditions.

- [Carhartt CT103828](/product.html?style=CT103828) — the rugged Duck Detroit jacket in Carhartt's duck canvas; it stands up to real work and real weather, and it's a favorite for crews that want their brand on gear that lasts. See [custom Carhartt](/custom-carhartt).
- [CornerStone CSV105](/product.html?style=CSV105) — an ANSI Class 2 surveyor vest for teams that need certified visibility on the road or the site. Browse [custom CornerStone](/custom-cornerstone) and our [safety apparel program](/custom-safety-apparel).

For heavier outerwear, we also decorate [Eddie Bauer](/custom-eddie-bauer) and [The North Face](/custom-north-face) jackets when you want a premium branded layer.

## Start Here, Then Build Out

You don't need to figure out all of this at once. Pick a cap, a tee, and one layer that fits your work, and you've got a lineup that covers most of what a PNW business needs. From there, add polos for the office and hi-vis for the crews.

Once you know which styles you want, we'll help you dial in decoration, colors, and quantities. Ready to move? [Request a quote](/pages/request-a-quote.html) and our team in Milton will get you real numbers and a mockup — no guesswork.
